vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (Yellow Pot) is devoid of visible plant matter. P3 (Black Pot with Rabbit) contains a single Pothos leaf, but the rabbit anchor is missing in the final frame. P2/P4 (Black Pot) shows a single, struggling leaf and significant debris.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Contains only a single, chlorotic leaf fragment and eggshell debris.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen is in a state of severe decline; only one leaf remains.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking drainage.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshells in P3/P4; a small electronic component (IC chip) appears on the desk surface in later images.
biome_observations.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor has been removed or displaced from the P3 pot between T-1 and CURRENT.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T-3: Minimal change; static state of decline.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Introduction of a paper receipt/document next to the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Removal of the receipt; introduction of an electronic component (IC chip) on the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Significant loss of visual clarity; disappearance of the white rabbit anchor; further degradation of the remaining leaf structure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal collapse. The lack of foliage, presence of debris, and loss of the scale anchor suggest a neglected environment where the original specimens have perished.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/debris
Electronic component (IC chip) on desk surface
Disappearance of the white rabbit scale anchor
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. What was once a collection of four distinct plants has been reduced to a single, struggling leaf fragment. The environment is cluttered with non-botanical debris, and the primary scale anchor (the rabbit) has been removed, indicating a total abandonment of the original horticultural intent.
